message:
fix(trace): guard TraceMonoid PartialEq against fingerprint hash collisions

The PartialEq impl for TraceMonoid previously delegated directly to
equivalent(), which compares only the 64-bit fingerprint. While collisions
are astronomically unlikely in practice, a hash-only equality check violates
PartialEq's contract (must reflect true structural equality) and could cause
silent correctness bugs in HashMap/BTreeMap lookups.

Now uses a two-phase check: fast-reject on fingerprint mismatch (avoiding
the more expensive canonical comparison when hashes differ), then falls
through to equivalent_exact() which compares the full FoataTrace canonical
forms layer-by-layer.

Added test proving the distinction: constructs a synthetic TraceMonoid with
a spoofed fingerprint matching another trace but different canonical layers,
and verifies that equivalent() returns true (fingerprint match) while
PartialEq (==) correctly returns false (structural mismatch).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
